summary: Trusted Cars — a modern, minimalistic pre-owned car dealership website for a company based in Karol Bagh, Delhi. White/Red/Black color theme, mobile responsive, with homepage, car listing, car detail, test drive booking, secure admin panel, and SEO optimization.
architectural_notes: Color theme: White, Red, Black — minimal premium UI, no theme changes
Sticky header with logo and phone number (9582149643); logo also in footer
All Call Now buttons use tel:+919582149643; WhatsApp floating button uses https://wa.me/919582149643
Admin panel at hidden route /admin — email/password login, not accessible to public users
Admin credentials: email = hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com, password = Hitesh1234@
Header/footer logo: user's custom logo displayed to the LEFT of the existing Trusted Cars wordmark logo — both shown together
known_issues: Add Car form in admin panel submits without error but car is not being saved — silent failure on addCar mutation

existing_features.title: Apply a clean, premium White/Red/Black design system across the entire site. Use white (#FFFFFF) as the primary background, deep black (#111111) for text and structural elements, and a vivid red (#E31B1B) as the accent/action color. Use a modern sans-serif font (Inter or similar via Google Fonts). Enforce consistent spacing, generous padding, and a minimal card/section aesthetic throughout all pages. The design must be fully mobile responsive with a hamburger menu on mobile.

Build a sticky header component that appears on all pages. It must include: the Trusted Cars logo (text-based or using the generated logo image) on the left, navigation links (Home, Cars, Book Test Drive) in the center, and the phone number '9582149643' as a clickable 'Call Now' link (tel:+919582149643) styled in red on the right. The header must remain fixed at the top when scrolling and have a white background with a subtle bottom shadow.

Build the Homepage with the following sections: (1) Hero section with headline 'Buy Trusted Pre-Owned Cars in Karol Bagh', subtext 'Certified • Verified • Transparent Pricing', two CTA buttons — 'View Cars' (navigates to /cars) and 'Book Test Drive' (navigates to /book) — styled with a full-width background using the generated hero image. (2) Featured Cars section that fetches and displays featured cars from the backend in a horizontal scroll or grid of cards. (3) Why Choose Us section with four items: Quality Check, Verified Cars, Easy Finance, Transparent Pricing — each with an icon. (4) Customer Reviews section with 3–4 static testimonial cards. (5) Clean footer with Trusted Cars logo, address (Karol Bagh, Delhi, India), phone (9582149643 as tel:+919582149643), and copyright.

Build a Car Listing page at route /cars. Display all cars in a responsive grid. Each car card must show: car image (first image from images array), brand & model, year, KM driven, fuel type, transmission, price (formatted in INR), EMI starting value, a status tag ('Available' in green / 'Sold' in red), and a 'View Details' button that navigates to /cars/:slug. Add a filter sidebar/panel with filters for: Budget (price range), Brand (dropdown), Fuel Type, Transmission, and Year. Filters should update the displayed results in real time on the client side.

Build a Car Detail page at route /cars/:slug. The page must fetch the car by slug from the backend and display: (1) an image gallery slider showing all car images with navigation arrows, (2) full specifications (brand, model, year, KM driven, fuel type, transmission, ownership), (3) price prominently displayed in INR with EMI breakdown, (4) a 'Finance Available' info section, (5) three action buttons: 'Book Test Drive' (navigates to /book with car pre-selected), 'Enquire Now' (opens a simple modal with a name/phone form), and 'Call Now' (tel:+919582149643). Set the page <title> and meta description dynamically per car, optimized for 'Used Cars in Karol Bagh'.

Build a Test Drive Booking page at route /book. The form must include: Name (required text input), Phone (required text input), Email (optional email input), Preferred Date (date picker, must be a future date), Preferred Time Slot (dropdown with options: 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM, 4:00 PM, 6:00 PM), and an optional Car selection dropdown pre-populated from the cars list. On submit, validate required fields, call the addBooking backend function, and show a success confirmation message: 'Your test drive has been booked! We will contact you shortly.' The form must be clean and minimal matching the site design.

Build a secure Admin Panel accessible only at route /admin. (1) Admin Login screen: email + password form. On submit, call adminLogin() backend function. If successful, store an 'isAdmin' flag in localStorage and redirect to /admin/dashboard. If failed, show error. (2) Admin Dashboard must include tabs/sections for: Car Management (list all cars with Edit, Delete, Mark as Sold actions; Add New Car form with all fields including multiple image URL inputs) and Booking Management (table of all bookings with columns: customer name, phone, email, car, date, time, status; option to update status; Export as CSV button that downloads booking data as a CSV file). (3) If not authenticated, redirect to /admin login. The /admin route must not be linked anywhere in the public navigation.

Add a floating WhatsApp button visible on all pages (fixed bottom-right). The button must link to https://wa.me/919582149643, open in a new tab, use the WhatsApp green color (#25D366), display a WhatsApp icon, and have a subtle hover animation. It must not overlap any critical UI on mobile.

Use the generated logo image (trusted-cars-logo.dim_200x60.png) in the sticky header and footer. Use the generated hero background image (hero-bg.dim_1440x700.png) in the homepage hero section as a full-width background with a dark overlay to ensure text readability.

In the Header component, display the user's uploaded logo image (user-logo.dim_200x60.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/) to the LEFT of the existing Trusted Cars wordmark/logo. Both logos must appear side by side. Do not remove or replace the existing Trusted Cars logo — only add the user's logo on its left. Apply the same change in the Footer component.

In the AdminLoginPage component, add detailed client-side logging (console.error) of the exact error returned by the adminLogin mutation to aid debugging. Also ensure the error message displayed to the user clearly distinguishes between a network/connection error and an invalid credentials error.

In the AdminLoginPage frontend component (frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminLoginPage.tsx), add a debug console.log before the mutation call that prints the exact email string and its length, and the exact password string and its length. This will confirm on the client side that no extra whitespace or invisible characters are being sent to the backend. Also ensure the form's onSubmit handler calls e.preventDefault() and that the input values are read via controlled state (not directly from DOM), to rule out any form-encoding issue.

In frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminLoginPage.tsx, after a failed login attempt, display the raw error details from the backend response in a visible debug panel on the page (not just in the console) so the user can see the exact failure reason. This panel should show: the error message string, and if available, the HTTP status code or canister rejection code. This panel is only shown when an error occurs and should be dismissible.

In the Admin Dashboard (frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminDashboardPage.tsx) and the CarForm component (frontend/src/components/admin/CarForm.tsx), replace the 'upload your car later' placeholder with a fully functional Add New Car form. The form must include all required fields: Title, Brand, Model, Year, KM Driven, Fuel Type (dropdown: Petrol/Diesel/CNG/Electric/Hybrid), Transmission (dropdown: Manual/Automatic), Ownership (dropdown: 1st/2nd/3rd/4th+), Price (INR), EMI (INR), Description (textarea), Status (dropdown: Available/Sold), Featured (checkbox/toggle), and multiple image URL inputs (allow adding/removing additional image URL fields dynamically, minimum 1). The slug must be auto-generated from Brand + Model + Year. On submit, validate all required fields, call the addCar backend mutation, close the form/modal on success, and show a success toast/message. The form must be visible and accessible from the Car Management tab in the admin dashboard.

In the CarForm component (frontend/src/components/admin/CarForm.tsx) and AdminDashboardPage (frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminDashboardPage.tsx), fix the addCar mutation call so that: (1) all form field values are correctly mapped to the exact parameter types and field names expected by the backend addCar function (e.g., Nat vs Text conversions for year, price, emi, kmDriven), (2) the mutation's onSuccess and onError callbacks are both implemented — onSuccess must invalidate the cars query and close the modal and show a success toast, and onError must display a visible error message to the user showing the reason for failure, (3) add console.log statements before the mutation call that print the full payload being sent so the mismatch can be diagnosed in the browser console.

Fix the admin authorization check in the backend addCar function. The error 'Unauthorized: Only admins can add cars' is shown even when the user is logged in as admin. The frontend must pass the admin session token/credential correctly when calling addCar, and the backend must correctly validate the admin identity. Audit the actor's authorization logic: ensure the admin token or caller identity passed from the frontend (via useActor hook / URL param token mechanism) is correctly received and validated in addCar before rejecting the call. The fix must ensure that a logged-in admin can successfully add a car without receiving an Unauthorized error.

Fix the Admin Login page so that it no longer requires an admin token to be present in the URL before allowing login. The error message 'Admin token not configured. Please access the admin panel via the correct URL with the admin token.' must be removed. The admin login flow must work by simply navigating to /admin and submitting valid credentials (email: hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com, password: Hitesh1234@) without any URL token prerequisite. Remove or bypass any token-presence check that blocks the login form submission.

Audit the useAdminActor hook and useQueries adminLogin mutation to ensure the admin actor is initialized correctly without depending on a URL-based token. The admin session must be established using only the credentials returned from the backend adminLogin call. After a successful adminLogin, the admin actor must be ready to make authenticated calls (e.g., addCar) without requiring a separately injected URL token.

new_feature_requests.summary: Homepage with hero section, featured cars, Why Choose Us, customer reviews, and footer
Car listing page with grid layout, car cards (image, specs, price, EMI, status), and filters (budget, brand, fuel, transmission, year)
Car detail page with image gallery slider, full specs, price breakdown, finance section, and action buttons (Book Test Drive, Enquire Now, Call Now)
Test drive booking system with form (name, phone, email, date, time slot), confirmation message, database save, and admin email notification
Secure admin panel at /admin with login, car management (add/edit/delete/sold/images), booking management, and export functionality
Database structure: Cars collection (title, brand, model, year, km, fuel, transmission, ownership, price, EMI, description, images, status, featured, slug) and Bookings collection
SEO optimization: friendly URLs, meta title/description per car, optimized for 'Used Cars in Karol Bagh'
WhatsApp floating button linked to https://wa.me/919582149643 and Call Now buttons using tel:+919582149643 throughout the site

Implement the backend data model and actor for Cars and Bookings. Cars record must include: id (Nat), title (Text), brand (Text), model (Text), year (Nat), kmDriven (Nat), fuelType (Text), transmission (Text), ownership (Text), price (Nat), emi (Nat), description (Text), images ([Text]), status (Text — 'Available' | 'Sold'), featured (Bool), slug (Text). Bookings record must include: id (Nat), customerName (Text), phone (Text), email (Text), carId (Nat), date (Text), timeSlot (Text), status (Text). Expose CRUD functions for both collections: addCar, updateCar, deleteCar, getCars, getCarBySlug, getFeaturedCars, markCarAsSold; addBooking, getBookings, updateBookingStatus. Also include admin authentication: a hardcoded admin credential check (adminLogin(email, password) -> Bool) that returns a session token or Bool. All state persists across upgrades using stable variables.
Set admin credentials: email hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com, password Hitesh1234@
Add user's uploaded logo to the left of the existing Trusted Cars wordmark logo in header and footer (keep both logos side by side)
Update the hardcoded admin credentials in the backend adminLogin function to use email 'hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com' and password 'Hitesh1234@'.

Fix the adminLogin function in the Motoko backend so that it correctly compares the provided email and password against the hardcoded credentials (email: 'hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com', password: 'Hitesh1234@') and returns true when both match. Investigate and resolve any issues such as whitespace trimming, text encoding differences, or incorrect comparison logic that would cause a valid credential pair to return false.

Completely rewrite the adminLogin function in the Motoko backend (backend/main.mo) to use a bulletproof byte-level equality check for the hardcoded credentials (email: 'hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com', password: 'Hitesh1234@'). Remove any prior comparison logic and replace it with a direct Text equality check using '==' after explicitly trimming any possible leading/trailing whitespace from both the input arguments and the stored constants. Add a debug-friendly variant: if the login fails, log (via Debug.print) the exact input values and their character counts alongside the stored values and their character counts, so the mismatch can be identified. The function must return true if and only if both email and password exactly match the stored credentials.

In backend/main.mo, completely replace the adminLogin function implementation with a minimal, direct hardcoded credential check: store the admin email as the exact literal string 'hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com' and the admin password as the exact literal string 'Hitesh1234@' inside the function body itself (not in stable variables). Use a single return statement: return (email == "hiteshmodi2007@gmail.com" and password == "Hitesh1234@"). Remove all prior comparison logic, stable credential variables, trimming, or helper calls. Add Debug.print statements before the comparison that log the exact input email, its size (Text.size), the exact input password, its size, and whether the comparison succeeded, so any mismatch can be diagnosed in canister logs.
Fix the Add New Car form in the admin panel — currently shows 'upload your car later' placeholder instead of a functional car upload/add form with all required fields and image URL inputs

In the backend (backend/main.mo), audit the addCar function to ensure it correctly accepts and persists a new car record to stable state. Verify that: (1) the function signature matches what the frontend is calling, (2) the car record is inserted into the stable map/array and is retrievable via getCars, (3) add Debug.print statements logging the incoming car data and the resulting map size after insertion so failures are visible in canister logs, and (4) the function returns a success value (e.g., the new car's id) that the frontend can use to confirm success.
